finding.summary: Track’s true peak is very close to 0 dBFS (-0.4 dB) while the overall loudness is moderate (-20 LUFS integrated), yielding a high crest factor. An enabled delay plugin on the track may be increasing peak levels.
finding.probable_cause: The enabled VST3: Generic Delay is feeding delayed copies back into the signal, potentially summing with the original pluck and raising transient peaks. Without parameter values, the exact contribution is unknown.
finding.confidence_reason: The effect of the delay on the peaks cannot be definitively quantified because its parameters (mix, feedback, time) are unavailable, but bypassing offers a direct test.

evidence_refs.description: True peak measured at -0.4 dBFS, only 0.4 dB below digital full scale.
Crest factor of 12 dB indicates large difference between peak and average levels.
Track FX chain contains an enabled VST3: Generic Delay with no exposed parameters in this capture.

proposal.reason: True peak is -0.4 dBFS; the enabled delay may be summing delayed repeats, raising peaks. Bypassing will reveal its contribution. This is a preview – the plugin stays in the chain but is temporarily disabled.
